conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances. Let me start by gathering some basic information.

Do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have three children - Alex who's 5, Blake who's 21, and Charlie who's 13.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. It's helpful to know you have three children - Alex (5), Blake (21), and Charlie (13).

Let me ask you: Is Blake (who is 21) currently in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't in any education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. 

Now, can you tell me - do all three children currently live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, all three of them live with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you.

Can you tell me - are you or your partner (if you have one) responsible for these children? And are you or your partner currently living or working in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm responsible for them, and I live here in the UK - in Manchester actually.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information.

Just a few more questions to complete the assessment:

Are any of the children currently in care (looked after by the local authority), or have any of them been in hospital for more than 12 weeks?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Charlie was in care for a little while - just a couple of months. But Alex hasn't been in care or hospital, and neither has Blake.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for sharing that. 

Can you tell me - is Charlie still in care now, or has Charlie returned home to live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Charlie is back home with me now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

One more question: Is anyone else claiming Child Benefit for any of these three children, or contributing more than you do towards their upkeep?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them, and I'm the one taking care of them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Let me just read the official guidance to ensure I give you the correct assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for your patience. Based on the information you've provided and the official UK government guidance, I can now provide you with an assessment of your Child Benefit eligibility for each of your three children.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Your Child Benefit Eligibility Assessment

Based on the information you've provided, here's the outcome for each of your children:

**Alex (5 years old): ✓ Eligible**
Alex is under 16, lives with you, and you're responsible for them. You meet all the criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

**Blake (21 years old): ✗ Not Eligible**
Unfortunately, Blake is not eligible for Child Benefit. The age limit is under 16, or under 20 if the child is in approved education or training. Since Blake is 21 and not currently in education or training, they don't meet the age requirements.

**Charlie (13 years old): ✓ Eligible**
Charlie is under 16 and now lives back at home with you. Although Charlie was in care for a couple of months, this doesn't affect your eligibility now that Charlie has returned home and you're responsible for them.

**Summary:** You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for **Alex and Charlie**. You can claim for both children at the same time.

Please note: If you or your partner's individual income is over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, though you can still claim the benefit.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 5 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 21 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 21, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Alex (5) and Charlie (13) both meet the eligibility criteria as they are under 16, live with the claimant, and no one else is claiming for them. Charlie's previous time in care does not affect current eligibility since Charlie has returned home. Blake (21) is not eligible as Blake exceeds the maximum age limit of 20 for those in approved education or training, and is not currently in any education or training.
